TF-E7.Summary: Option A: Hybrid Deploy — Vercel Frontend + Docker Backend E2E
TF-E7.Description:
Complete hybrid deployment: Vercel-hosted Next.js frontend, Docker backend on GHCR with nginx + supervisord + FastAPI + Redis + stdio MCP, Supabase production wiring, and end-to-end verification across environments.

SCOPE:
- Production Dockerfile for backend-only image (frontend on Vercel)
- nginx TLS termination and API reverse proxy for Docker backend
- supervisord: fastapi, redis-sidecar or external Redis, mcp-postgres stdio
- Vercel project config with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
- docker-compose local parity stack
- E2E: Vercel FE → Docker BE → Supabase → MCP stdio

EFFORT: M (30 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-E1 minimum; ideally TF-E3 for MCP E2E

EPIC LINK: epic/taskflow-implementation
FEATURE BRANCH: epic/TF-E7-hybrid-deploy

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/TaskFlowAI_Project_Proposal.md (§ Deployment Strategy)
- docs/guidance/docker-setup.md (when created)
- docs/guidance/supabase-setup.md (when created)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Frontend live on Vercel; backend on GHCR accessible via HTTPS
- Local docker-compose mirrors production backend topology
- E2E: login on Vercel → CRUD tasks → AI parse-task (if MVP 3 done)
- MCP stdio PostgreSQL returns tasks in containerized backend

Create multi-stage Dockerfile for FastAPI backend, nginx, supervisord — frontend deploys separately to Vercel.

USE CASES:
- CI builds backend image pushed to GHCR
- Container runs FastAPI on 8010 internally, nginx on 80/443 externally
- Image includes Node.js 22 for npx MCP stdio servers

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Update infrastructure/Dockerfile from TF-012 scaffold
- Stage 1: python:3.12-slim, uv sync --frozen, copy backend/, alembic/
- Stage 2: production — install nginx, supervisor, nodejs 22
- COPY nginx.conf, supervisord.conf
- EXPOSE 80; CMD supervisord
- Non-root user where possible
- Document in docs/guidance/docker-setup.md

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-012

TESTING CRITERIA:
- docker build -f infrastructure/Dockerfile succeeds
- Container /health returns 200 via nginx
- Image size documented <1GB target

EDGE CASES:
- alembic.ini copied for migration job sidecar pattern
- .dockerignore excludes frontend/node_modules and .venv
- Python slim lacks gcc: use wheels only

Configure nginx reverse proxy and supervisord for FastAPI, MCP, and optional Redis in production container.

USE CASES:
- External HTTPS terminates at nginx → /api/* proxied to FastAPI
- supervisord restarts crashed FastAPI or MCP processes
- /health and /metrics accessible for load balancer probes

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create infrastructure/nginx.conf: proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8010 for /api/, /health, /metrics
- proxy_read_timeout 120s for AI endpoints
- Create infrastructure/supervisord.conf:
  - [program:nginx] priority=10
  - [program:fastapi] uv run uvicorn backend.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8010
  - [program:mcp-postgres] npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres (stdio managed by FastAPI spawn OR separate — document pattern)
- TLS: document cert mount for prod; self-signed for local

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-063

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All supervisord programs stay running 5 min soak test
- nginx proxies API without 502
- WebSocket headers if needed for future

EDGE CASES:
- MCP stdio: spawn per-request from FastAPI vs supervisord persistent — use per-request spawn per daily-briefing pattern
- Port conflict: nginx 80 only external
- Log rotation: stdout to docker logs

Create docker-compose.yml for local backend + Redis with env parity to production.

USE CASES:
- Developer runs docker-compose up for full backend locally while frontend on npm run dev
- NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost/api/v1 points to compose nginx
- Redis available for rate limit and session tests

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- infrastructure/docker-compose.yml: services app (build Dockerfile), redis
- env_file: .env; ports 80:80, 6379:6379 optional
- healthcheck on /health
- volumes: none for prod parity (optional mount for dev hot reload documented separately)
- Document try-it-locally.md workflow: terminal 1 compose, terminal 2 frontend dev

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-064

TESTING CRITERIA:
- docker-compose up healthy within 90s
- Frontend dev server CRUD against localhost API works
- Redis ping from FastAPI container succeeds

EDGE CASES:
- Port 80 in use: document COMPOSE_PORT=8080 override
- .env missing: compose fails with clear message
- Apple Silicon: platform linux/amd64 if needed

Configure Vercel project for Next.js 16 frontend with production API URL and CORS alignment.

USE CASES:
- Push to main deploys frontend to Vercel preview/production
- NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL points to Docker backend HTTPS URL
- CORS on backend allows Vercel preview URLs

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- vercel.json: framework nextjs, env vars documentation
- Backend CORS: allow VERCEL_URL pattern and production domain
- Create docs/guidance/vercel-setup.md: project link, env vars, domain config
- GitHub integration for preview deployments per PR
- Security headers on Vercel: CSP, X-Frame-Options

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend, Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-004, TF-003

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Vercel preview build succeeds
- Production API call from Vercel domain succeeds (CORS)
- Environment variables set in Vercel dashboard documented

EDGE CASES:
- Preview URL wildcard CORS: restrict to *.vercel.app pattern
- API URL http vs https: enforce https in production
- Server Components fetching API: use server-side env not exposed secret

TF-E7.Summary: PostgreSQL MCP stdio and Supervisord Integration
TF-E7.Description:
Wire stdio PostgreSQL MCP for production Docker with Supabase connection.

USE CASES:
- Context Agent in Docker container reads tasks via stdio MCP
- MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio in production .env
- Supavisor :6543 connection with statement_cache_size=0

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Finalize backend/mcp/postgres_stdio.py from TF-023 for container env
- supervisord or FastAPI subprocess spawn npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres
- Env: MCP_POSTGRES_URL, DATABASE_URL, MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio
- Node 22 in Dockerfile for npx
- Update docs/MCP.md with TaskFlow tool allowlist
- SSL/TLS to Supabase documented

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-023, TF-063

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Context agent success in container against Supabase seeded data
- MCP_TRANSPORT=http mock still works in unit tests
- Connection string special chars handled

EDGE CASES:
- npx cold start 5-10s: acceptable, log warning
- Supabase pooler: prepared statement cache disabled
- MCP crash mid-request: escalate mcp_timeout

End-to-end verification of Vercel + Docker + Supabase stack and complete setup guides.

USE CASES:
- New developer follows docs/guidance/docker-setup.md and has working hybrid stack in <1 hour
- E2E test: Vercel FE login → create task → AI suggest priority (if MVP 3)
- Portfolio demo URL documented in README

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create docs/guidance/docker-setup.md, update try-it-locally.md, supabase-setup.md
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_hybrid.sh or pytest e2e marker against staging URLs
- Verify CORS, auth cookies/cross-origin, trace_id end-to-end
- Update docs/PLAN.md TF-E7 complete
- README deployment section with architecture diagram

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Testing, Documentation
DEPENDENCIES: TF-063, TF-064, TF-065, TF-066, TF-067

TESTING CRITERIA:
- E2E script exits 0 against staging
- All guidance docs non-empty with copy-paste commands
- README links Vercel + backend URLs

EDGE CASES:
- E2E without Vercel: test FE locally against Docker BE still passes
- Staging credentials in GitHub secrets only
- AI E2E skipped if MVP 3 not merged: CRUD-only E2E still required
